title: Mediterranean Chickpea Salad

ingredients.name: cooked chickpeas (canned, drained) or boiled kabuli chana

subs.name: Canned butter beans or lobia (black-eyed peas) give a different but equally satisfying result; firm cubed paneer can be added alongside chickpeas for extra protein
Fresh coriander leaves (dhania) are an excellent Indian substitute and arguably even better in this salad given the cumin in the dressing
Any good-quality oil works; the olive oil is used raw as a dressing ingredient so it has noticeable flavour impact
A small pinch of roasted cumin powder (bhuna jeera) from any Indian pantry is actually a superb choice here — the toasting amplifies the flavour

steps.text: If using canned chickpeas, drain them in a sieve and rinse well under cold water to remove any tinned taste. If using dried kabuli chana, soak them overnight and pressure-cook for 3–4 whistles until tender, then drain and cool completely before using.

Make the dressing in a large bowl: whisk together the olive oil, lemon juice, cumin, and a generous pinch of salt until combined. Taste and adjust — it should be bright, tangy, and well-seasoned before any vegetables go in.

Add the chickpeas to the dressing and toss to coat. Add the diced tomatoes, cucumber, red onion, and parsley. Fold everything together gently — you want the chickpeas to stay intact rather than getting crushed. Add the mint if using.

Taste the salad and adjust — it may need more salt, more lemon, or more cumin depending on the acidity of your tomatoes and lemon. Optionally warm a flatbread or roti in a dry skillet and serve alongside for scooping. The salad keeps well in the refrigerator for up to 2 days; the chickpeas absorb the dressing and become even more flavourful overnight.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.

substitution_notes: chickpea → Canned butter beans or lobia (black-eyed peas) give a different but equally satisfying result; firm cubed paneer can be added alongside chickpeas for extra protein; parsley → Fresh coriander leaves (dhania) are an excellent Indian substitute and arguably even better in this salad given the cumin in the dressing; olive-oil → Any good-quality oil works; the olive oil is used raw as a dressing ingredient so it has noticeable flavour impact; cumin → A small pinch of roasted cumin powder (bhuna jeera) from any Indian pantry is actually a superb choice here — the toasting amplifies the flavour
indian_kitchen_adaptation: Kabuli chana (white chickpeas) is the same ingredient as the chickpeas used across the Mediterranean. Canned chickpeas are increasingly available at Indian supermarkets and save considerable time; otherwise boil soaked chickpeas in a pressure cooker for 3–4 whistles. All other ingredients — cucumber, tomato, onion, mint, lemon, cumin — are staples of the Indian kitchen. The cumin in the dressing (bhuna jeera) can be dry-roasted and ground fresh for a more intense flavour. This makes an ideal tiffin box salad as it holds well for hours without wilting.
